title: Getting random 403 Forbidden errors on the dashboard
body: I keep getting 403 errors when I try to access the TaskFlow dashboard. It's not every time — sometimes it loads fine, sometimes I get a 403 page. It seems totally random. My permissions haven't changed and other people on my team say they're seeing it too. Started happening a couple days ago.

comments.author: triage-agent
comments.body:
Thanks for reporting this — intermittent 403s are frustrating. A couple of things that would help narrow this down:

When you hit the 403, are you seeing it as a full error page in the browser, or are parts of the dashboard loading while specific API calls fail? If you're able to open your browser's DevTools (Network tab) next time it happens, the exact URL that returns the 403 and any response body would be really valuable.

comments.author: reporter
comments.body: It's a full error page — the whole page just shows 403 Forbidden, nothing else loads. I haven't tried the DevTools thing but I can try next time it happens. It's weird because if I just refresh the page it usually works again after a couple tries. I kind of assumed it was a network issue on our end but my coworker who also just got the new analyst role is seeing the same thing.

comments.author: triage-agent
comments.body: That's a really helpful detail — you mentioned you and your coworker both recently got the new analyst role. Do you know if anyone *without* that new analyst role is also seeing these 403 errors? And roughly when was the analyst role assigned — was it around the same time the 403s started a couple days ago?

comments.author: reporter
comments.body: Now that you mention it, I think the analyst role was set up right around when this started — like two days ago maybe? And yeah, I asked a couple other people on the team who have the regular editor role and they said they haven't had any issues. So it does seem like it might be related to the analyst role specifically. That's kind of weird though because my permissions should be fine — our admin said the role was all set up.

comments.author: triage-agent
comments.body: That's really useful — the analyst role timing lines up perfectly. The part I'm trying to figure out is why it would come and go rather than just being broken all the time. Can you tell me: when you hit the 403, does it tend to happen right when you first open the dashboard (like at the start of your day or after being away for a while), or does it also happen mid-session while you're already using it? And is it always the main dashboard URL, or have you noticed it on other pages within TaskFlow too?

comments.author: reporter
comments.body: It happens pretty much anytime — not just when I first open it. Like I could be working fine, click to another page, and bam, 403. Then I refresh and it's fine again. It's not just the dashboard either, I've gotten it on other pages too. It really does seem random, like maybe 1 out of every 3 times I load a page it happens? I keep thinking it's some kind of network glitch but it's been going on for two days now so that seems unlikely.

comments.author: triage-agent
comments.body: That pattern is really telling — thanks for walking through it. One more thing that will help us point a developer in the right direction: when you see the 403 page, does it look like a TaskFlow-styled page (with the app's logo, colors, or layout), or is it a plain/generic error page — maybe just white text on a blank background, or something that looks like it could come from a web server like nginx or Apache? If you can grab a screenshot next time it happens, that would be ideal, but even a rough description of what the 403 page looks like would help.

comments.author: reporter
comments.body: Hmm, I'm trying to remember exactly. I don't think it's a TaskFlow-styled page — it's pretty plain looking. Like just the text "403 Forbidden" on a mostly blank page. I think there might be something small at the bottom mentioning nginx but I'm not 100% sure on that. It definitely doesn't have the TaskFlow logo or anything like that. I'll try to grab a screenshot next time it pops up.

comments.author: triage-agent
comments.body: [RESOLVED] {"title":"Intermittent nginx-level 403 errors for users with the new analyst role — likely inconsistent role config across load-balanced instances","problem":"Users assigned the newly created 'analyst' role receive intermittent 403 Forbidden errors (roughly 1 in 3 page loads) across all TaskFlow pages. The 403 is served by nginx (plain error page, not application-styled), and a page refresh typically resolves it temporarily. Users with the 'editor' role are unaffected. The issue began approximately two days ago, coinciding with the analyst role's creation.","root_cause_hypothesis":"TaskFlow runs behind a load balancer with multiple nginx instances (or upstream servers). When the analyst role was created, its permission/access configuration was applied to some but not all instances. Requests routed to a correctly configured instance succeed; requests routed to an unconfigured instance return a 403 from nginx before the request ever reaches the application. The ~1-in-3 failure rate is consistent with one of three backend servers missing the configuration.","reproduction_steps":["Log in as a user with the analyst role","Navigate to the TaskFlow dashboard or any other page","Refresh the page repeatedly (roughly 5-10 times)","Observe that approximately 1 in 3 loads returns a plain nginx 403 Forbidden page","Confirm that the same pages load consistently for a user with the editor role"],"environment":"Production TaskFlow instance, users with the analyst role (created ~2 days ago), nginx reverse proxy / load balancer in front of the application","severity":"high","impact":"All users with the analyst role are affected. They can work around the issue by refreshing the page, but the frequent 403 errors significantly disrupt their workflow. The number of affected users is at least two but likely includes all analysts.","recommended_fix":"1. Check the nginx configuration across all load-balanced instances/servers for analyst role access rules — look for inconsistencies. 2. If using a config management or deployment tool, verify the analyst role config was deployed to all instances. 3. Reload or re-deploy the corrected nginx configuration to all instances. 4. If permissions are managed at the API gateway or reverse proxy layer (e.g., nginx auth_request, map directives, or upstream ACLs), ensure the analyst role is recognized consistently. 5. Verify the fix by logging in as an analyst-role user and confirming no 403s across 20+ page loads.","proposed_test_case":"Automated test: Make 50 sequential authenticated HTTP requests as an analyst-role user to the dashboard endpoint and assert that all return 200. Run this against each individual backend instance (bypassing the load balancer) to confirm per-instance correctness.","information_gaps":["Exact number of backend nginx instances behind the load balancer","How the analyst role permissions are configured at the nginx layer (auth_request module, map directive, external auth service, etc.)","Total number of users affected (only two confirmed so far)","Whether the 403 response includes any additional headers or body content beyond the plain error text"]}
